purpose: Define the semantic, timing, acoustic, runtime, and theme-latitude obligations that audio ingestion and conformance tooling must satisfy before runtime cue promotion.

philosophy.topLevelGoal: Treat audio as a gameplay language: every cue should tell the player what happened, when it happened, how important it is, and whether the game state changed.
philosophy.conformanceGoal: Support highly conformant Galaga-like realizations while preserving a documented lane for genre-faithful Aurora variants and later theme packs.
philosophy.ingestionGoal: Make cue contracts repeatable for future games so reference clips, segmentation, candidate generation, runtime validation, and theme latitude can be derived before subjective tuning.

scoringLayers.label: Semantic Meaning
scoringLayers.meaning: The gameplay message the player should understand from the cue.
scoringLayers.measuredBy: audio event matrix coverage, semantic event score, distinct-from relationships

scoringLayers.label: Timing Slot
scoringLayers.meaning: The trigger, cooldown, stop behavior, overlap budget, and allowed tail length.
scoringLayers.measuredBy: audio cue alignment, runtime event logs, transition overlap analysis

scoringLayers.label: Acoustic Identity
scoringLayers.meaning: Envelope, duration, spectral centroid, band shape, noise/tone split, and segment role fit.
scoringLayers.measuredBy: audio theme comparison, event-gap segment risk, focused candidate sweeps

scoringLayers.label: Runtime Context
scoringLayers.meaning: Whether the cue survives actual gameplay mix pressure without masking adjacent events.
scoringLayers.measuredBy: promotion precheck, live runtime recapture, quality score, semantic regression guard

scoringLayers.label: Theme Latitude
scoringLayers.meaning: What may vary in Aurora or future theme packs while preserving arcade genre meaning.
scoringLayers.measuredBy: contract declared lane, required invariants, forbidden changes, variant allowances

referenceConformant.goal: Use measured reference windows or close synthetic equivalents.
referenceConformant.allowed: Small gain, cooldown, and window trimming changes when they improve runtime clarity.
referenceConformant.forbidden: Changing semantic class, reversing emotional meaning, masking adjacent critical cues, or stretching a transient into a musical flourish.

genreConformant.goal: Stay arcade-faithful even when the exact reference waveform is not copied.
genreConformant.allowed: Synthesis, chip-like remixes, alternate pitch centers, and small phrase variations that preserve event class and timing slot.
genreConformant.forbidden: Ambient pads, long cinematic tails, or modern UI sounds that break fixed-screen arcade readability.

auroraVariant.goal: Permit Aurora identity and later theme packs while preserving cue contract semantics.
auroraVariant.allowed: Harmonic color, staged theme shifts, brighter/lower variants, and musical motifs that keep the cue legible in play.
auroraVariant.forbidden: Any variant that makes hit vs kill, loss vs reward, or pressure vs transition ambiguous.

candidateCanOnlyBecomeTrialWhen: focused candidate gates pass or the cue contract explicitly marks the candidate as a composite trial
the full-theme promotion precheck allows a runtime trial
semantic score is expected to hold
the candidate does not increase overlap or masking risk by contract
candidateCanOnlyBePromotedWhen: runtime recapture improves or holds event gap for the target cue
average worst-segment risk holds or improves
semantic audio score does not regress
audio cue alignment remains fully green
overall quality remains at or above the release gate
the accepted decision and rejected alternatives are persisted

cueContracts.playerMeaning: The alien rack is alive, organized, and exerting pressure even when no shot or dive is occurring.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: This is the board heartbeat; it shapes perceived difficulty and must not become decorative ambience.

eventShape.trigger: starts after formation arrival and repeats during standard-stage play
eventShape.cooldownPolicy: cadence must preserve pressure without masking shots, hits, or transition cues
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must duck or stop for stageTransition, challengeTransition, challengeResults, and challengePerfect

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: short bright cadence body with a convincing onset and rack-pressure feel

ingestionRequirements: measure masking against shots and explosions
nextLearningQuestions: Is the reference window too narrow for formation pressure?
Do we need a ducking/mix model before tuning tone?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: A shot connected but may not have destroyed the target.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: The cue should separate contact from final destruction so the player understands damage hierarchy.

eventShape.trigger: fires immediately on standard enemy hit
eventShape.cooldownPolicy: short cooldown; should tolerate rapid shot cadence
eventShape.overlapPolicy: may overlap lightly with enemyBoom only if hit/kill distinction remains legible

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: sharp damage transient distinct from the destruction phrase

variantAllowances: small pitch/gain shifts by enemy family

ingestionRequirements: segment damage onset from Zako destruction
score distinctness against enemyBoom and bossHit
nextLearningQuestions: Do boss/multi-hit enemies need a separate damage layer?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: A normal enemy was destroyed and points were earned.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: The cue should feel final but smaller than boss destruction.

eventShape.cooldownPolicy: short enough for dense challenge-stage kills
eventShape.overlapPolicy: can overlap with score feedback, but must not bury playerShot or playerHit

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: measured Zako destruction burst with dry arcade tail

variantAllowances: enemy-family color and small gain scaling

ingestionRequirements: score tail masking in dense kill sequences
nextLearningQuestions: Should challenge-stage dense kills use a lower gain or grouped limiter?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: A boss or stronger target took damage but may still be alive.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: This cue teaches multi-hit state without confusing it with final destruction.

eventShape.overlapPolicy: should not mask bossBoom or capture cues

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: heavier damage confirmation with boss hierarchy

ingestionRequirements: score role distinction against enemyHit and bossBoom
nextLearningQuestions: Should visual damage flash and bossHit audio share a timing contract?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: A boss was destroyed; this should sound more consequential than a normal kill.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: This is combat hierarchy feedback and reward punctuation.

eventShape.overlapPolicy: can dominate nearby enemyBoom but not playerHit or capture/rescue state changes

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: larger boss-death burst with finality and dry arcade tail

ingestionRequirements: score against enemyBoom size and tail
measure overlap in escort kill clusters
nextLearningQuestions: Does bossBoom need a score-surface synchronization marker?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: The captured fighter was rescued and rejoined; this is a major positive state change.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: The cue must reward skill and clearly announce dual-fighter restoration.

eventShape.cooldownPolicy: long; should not retrigger during the same rescue sequence
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must not be confused with challengePerfect or capturedFighterDestroyed

ingestionRequirements: require full-theme precheck plus live recapture
nextLearningQuestions: Can reference subclip promotion work, or should this stay a synthesized arcade reward motif?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: The player lost a ship; this should be unmistakable and emotionally negative.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: Ship loss is a composite event and must align audio, explosion, remaining-ship state, and restart pacing.

eventShape.trigger: fires immediately on player ship destruction
eventShape.cooldownPolicy: long; one cue per loss event
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must dominate enemyBoom/bossBoom but not smear into gameOver or next-life restart

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: longer death cue with separable onset/body/tail

variantAllowances: small timbre changes by theme if segment roles remain clear

nextLearningQuestions: Should the player death implementation become a scheduled multi-part cue rather than one clipped sample?

cueContracts.playerMeaning: The player perfectly cleared a challenge stage; this is special and should exceed the standard result cue.
cueContracts.designerMeaning: Perfect-clear audio should reinforce mastery and high-score pursuit without delaying next-stage pacing.

eventShape.trigger: fires shortly after perfect challenge clear
eventShape.overlapPolicy: must stop or avoid stagePulse and challengeTransition; must leave clean handoff to next stage

targetGrounding.referenceExpectation: celebratory result phrase distinct from normal challenge results

variantAllowances: theme-colored flourish if timing and result hierarchy remain

ingestionRequirements: score phrase timing against challenge clear and next-stage cue
nextLearningQuestions: Do we need separate perfect-result scoring for audio and score-surface animation?
